nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Pyne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Pine  . This name has been established in Ireland since the 16th century.

variants.name: Pine
meanings: English: from Middle English pin(e) (Old English pīn, Old French pin), a topographic name for someone who lived by a conspicuous pine tree or in a pine forest; in some cases it may originally have been a nickname for a tall man, one thought to resemble a pine tree.
English: nickname from Middle English pine ‘pain, injury, torture, punishment, infirmity’, referring to someone who suffered from an inflicted pain or punishment.
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Le Pin in Calvados or some other French place called from a prominent pine.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William De Pyne I was born in 1159, in West Down, Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, Alexander De Pyne, was 25 and his mother, Mrs Alexander De Pyne, was 21. He married Juliana of Bradwell in 1182, in West Down, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1225, in his hometown, at the age of 66.

Il a épousé Juliana of Bradwell en 1182, à West Down, Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1225, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 66 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: William De Pyne Iが1159年にイギリスイギリスデボンウエスト ダウンで生まれた時，父Alexander De Pyneは25歳，母Mrs Alexander De Pyneは21歳であった。1182年にイギリスデボンウエスト ダウンでJuliana of Bradwellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1225年に故郷で66歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William De Pyne I(이)가 1159년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 웨스트 다운에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alexander De Pyne(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs Alexander De Pyne(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1182년 영국 데폰 웨스트 다운에서 Juliana of Bradwell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1225년 그의 고향에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.
